sections.name: A Markov Decision Process with full observability (identity observation model)
This model tests the MDP special case where A is identity — agent always knows its state
A: 4x4 Identity — each state maps deterministically to its own observation
D: Uniform prior — agent starts uncertain about position
State inference: qs = softmax(ln(A[o,:]) + ln(B[s_prev] @ pi))

sections.name: A classic multi-armed bandit formulated as a degenerate POMDP
Single hidden state context, multiple actions yield stochastic rewards
Tests the case where state dynamics are trivial but action-observation mapping matters
A: Reward likelihood. Context 0 → arm 0 best, context 1 → arm 1 best, etc
Columns are contexts, rows are reward observations (none/small/big)
B: Context is sticky regardless of action (arms don't change the world)
C: Strongly prefer big reward (obs 2), mildly prefer small (obs 1)
D: Uniform prior — don't know which arm is best
qs = infer_states(reward_obs) — learn which context/arm is active
G(pi) = EFE(pi) — trade off pragmatic (reward) and epistemic (info gain) value
u ~ softmax(-G) — select arm balancing exploration vs exploitation

sections.name: GNN Example: Active Inference POMDP Agent

This file is machine-readable and specifies a classic Active Inference agent for a discrete POMDP with one observation modality and one hidden state factor. The model is suitable for rendering into various simulation or inference backends.
A: 3 observations x 3 hidden states. Identity mapping (each state deterministically produces a unique observation). Rows are observations, columns are hidden states.

B: 3 states x 3 previous states x 3 actions. Each action deterministically moves to a state. For each slice, rows are previous states, columns are next states. Each slice is a transition matrix corresponding to a different action selection.
C: 3 observations. Preference in terms of log-probabilities over observations.
D: 3 states. Uniform prior over hidden states. Rows are hidden states, columns are prior probabilities.
E: 3 actions. Uniform habit used as initial policy prior.
Standard Active Inference update equations for POMDPs:
- State inference using Variational Free Energy with infer_states()
- Policy inference using Expected Free Energy = with infer_policies()
- Action selection from policy posterior: action = sample_action()
- Belief updating using Variational Free Energy with update_beliefs()

sections.name: GNN Example: Hidden Markov Model Baseline
Simple HMM for comparison with Active Inference POMDP variants.
Forward algorithm: alpha_t(s) = sum_{s'} P(o_t|s) * P(s|s') * alpha_{t-1}(s')
Backward algorithm: beta_t(s) = sum_{s'} P(o_{t+1}|s') * B(s'|s) * beta_{t+1}(s')
State posterior: Q(s_t) = alpha_t(s) * beta_t(s) / Z
The minimal possible POMDP: 2 hidden states, 2 observations, 2 actions
Tests edge-case handling for minimal state spaces
B: 2 actions. Action 0 = push left, action 1 = push right
G(pi) = EFE(pi) — Expected Free Energy per policy

variables.name: Observations
variables.definition: Observations = states directly (identity mapping for monitoring).

variables.name: type
variables.definition: type=float]    # Observation model (identity for direct monitoring)

sections.name: A simple discrete-time Markov Chain with no actions and no observation model
Tests the simplest possible dynamical system: just state transitions
Transition matrix: B[states_next, states_previous] (no action dimension)
A: 3x3 Identity — states are directly observed
B: 3x3 weather transition matrix (no action dimension)
Sunny tends to stay sunny. Cloudy can go anywhere. Rainy tends to stay rainy
Markov Chain evolution: s(t+1) ~ Categorical(B @ s(t))
No inference or action selection needed — passive system

sections.name: GNN Example: T-Maze Epistemic Foraging Agent
Classic Active Inference T-maze demonstrating epistemic foraging behavior
Location likelihood: identity mapping (agent knows its location)
Reward likelihood depends on location × context
At center or cue: no reward visible
At left arm: reward if context=reward_left
At right arm: reward if context=reward_right
At cue location: cue_left shown if context=reward_left
Preferences: strongly prefer reward, indifferent to no-reward or cue
Prior: starts at center, uncertain about context
G_epi(pi) = -E_Q[H[P(o|s)]] = expected information gain about hidden context
G_ins(pi) = -E_Q[log P(C|o)] = expected reward under policy
The epistemic component drives the agent to visit the cue location
to reduce uncertainty about context before committing to an arm
